Petition For Return Of Property; Conversion; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; For An Accounting; Double Damages; And Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Mar-01-2021 Continued To May-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
PES-19-303300 The Estate of Juan W. Sabada and
PTR-20-303542 In the matter of the Cantillano Irrevocable Trust

Appearances:
Jennifer McGuire (via Zoom) for and with Anthony Cantillano Sabada (via (Zoom).
Nancy Lofdahl (via Zoom) for Berta Quezada.

Ms. Lofdahl stated the 850 petition in the estate case can be resolved with a half day mediation. Ms. McGuire stated she would like a single mediation for all matters and would like to see the 850 petition granted. Ms. Lofdahl stated her client opposes the 850 petition, that the assets are not her client's, that her client was holding the assets for her brother, that none of the children are interested in it and it should go back to the estate for the one child in the estate matter. The Court noted Ms. McGuire's position that no written objections were filed. Ms. Lofdahl stated her client does object, that she is making oral objections now and the best way to resolve is mediation. Ms. McGuire stated Ms. Lofdahl has already conceded that the money should go back to the estate, that she would be happy to mediate both matters, and it would save time and money to deal with both matters at once instead of mediating on just one. Ms. Lofdahl stated that she can communicate with Ms. McGuire regarding her client's fees for helping her brother, that her client assisted her brother with his real properties and his court cases to clarify his name, provided tax support, provided assistance with his immigration status/name correction to collect his pension, that he was dependent on her and she should get compensation from the sale of these real properties.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than May 3, 2021. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May 10, 2021. The petitions in both the Estate and Trust cases are continued to May 17, 2021, and the Parties are to return on May 17, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Return Of Property; Conversion; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; For An Accounting; Double Damages; And Attorneys' Fees

Petition For Return Of Property; Conversion; Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; Constructive Fraud; Intentional Interference With Expected Inheritance; For An Accounting; Double Damages; And Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Jan-13-2021 Continued To Feb-10-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel for and Anthony Sabada.

The Court noted that no one is here to object, that it has reviewed the recent submissions and that it is satisfied on the proof as to Shawna. Mr. Wrobel stated that he can get some proof of subscribing witnesses. The Court instructed Mr. Wrobel to have it filed by February 13, 2020. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Mr. Wrobel stated that the will does not require bond. Status on close of estate is set for August 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration needs to be filed by January 28, 2021. If there is an issue with bond, Mr. Wrobel will be notified by the Probate Examiner.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-22-2020 Continued To Feb-05-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to February 5, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will have the Probate Examiner contact the Attorney.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Continued To Dec-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel for and with Anthony Sabada.

Mr. Wrobel stated that Shawna is not a minor, that it was a typo in the will, that he has a photo of Shawna's identification showing that she was born in June of 2001, that he filed a declaration last night and that this will need to be continued. The Court instructed Counsel to file a declaration with documents attached. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to December 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.
